storageDuplication.recommendation: Keep one learned memory per scenario/failure group. Move raw score-ref history into a compact event ledger when scoreRefReuseSlots or saturatedGroupCount rises, and keep only exemplar refs on the memory packet.

policy.identity: Learned memories are keyed by bank + scenario family + failure class, not by exact question wording.
policy.dedupe: Repeated score refs are ignored. New score refs update the same memory group instead of creating answer-key duplicates.
policy.vectorization: The vectorCandidates list is an optional middle index. JSONL memory remains authoritative.
policy.reinforcement: Once a memory group is saturated, new matching score refs should reinforce counters or event history rather than expand the agent-facing packet.

smartDedupePlan: Use scenarioId + failureClass as the semantic identity for learned MSP runbook memories.
Hash the normalized SOP summary, triggers, firstActions, decisionGates, and avoid list to detect same-lesson reinforcement.
Store repeated score evidence in a per-bank learning event ledger, then keep only top exemplar refs and aggregate counters in memory_units.jsonl.
Treat saturated memories as retrieval priorities with decay/replay metadata, not as text that should keep growing.
Vectorize compact chunks from vectorCandidates only as an optional middle index; never make vectors the authoritative record.

vectorCandidates.title: Learned: Authority and role activation (Backup/restore destruction incident)
vectorCandidates.chunkText: Learned: Authority and role activation (Backup/restore destruction incident) Judge-learned MSP SOP delta for Backup/restore destruction incident: Broad multi-tenant, destructive, regulated, or control-plane events require named incident authority instead of a solo overnight engineer. This is a failure-class memory, not an answer key. It was reinforced by 42 judge observation(s) across 15 run(s). Adaptive replay weight 10.0; next replay interval 30 day(s). Case family: Backup/restore destruction incident. Source case: msp-hard-backup-console-destruction. Observed judge pressure: - direct-anthropic-sonnet-open: Legal/compliance escalation thresholds are only partly explicit, and the plan could more clearly vet internal systems (ticketing/identity) as truly out-of-band before relying on them as the source of truth. - direct-minimax-highspeed-open: It provides no incident plan, no tenant separation, no evidence-preservation steps, no escalation path, and no service-continuity handling. - direct-openai-mini-open: Customer/legal/compliance communication thresholds are less fully developed than the technical/evidence workflow, and some evidence asks (for example token/session detail capt...
